channel.title: AP News 全文 RSS（简体中文版）
channel.description: AP News 首页抓取 + 英译简

item.title: 英国乔治王子选择伊顿公学作为他教育的下一步
item.description:
伦敦（美联社）——肯辛顿宫周二表示，英国乔治王子将于今年秋天追随父亲威廉王子的脚步进入伊顿公学。
几个月来，关于王位第二顺位继承人将在哪里继续接受教育的猜测一直很激烈，一些观察家预测他会选择马尔伯勒学院，这是他的母亲凯瑟琳公主就读的机构。
肯辛顿宫表示：“肯辛顿宫可以确认乔治王子将从今年九月开始就读伊顿公学。”
伊顿公学是一所由国王亨利六世于 1440 年创立的寄宿学校，以培养英国未来领导人而自豪，其中包括前首相罗伯特·沃波尔、大卫·卡梅伦和鲍里斯·约翰逊。除了他的父亲之外，乔治的叔叔哈里王子和他的叔祖父查尔斯·斯宾塞伯爵也曾在这所学校就读，学生们仍然穿着传统的燕尾服、硬挺的白领和细条纹裤子的校服。
12 岁的乔治目前就读于伯克郡的一所私立预科学校 Lambrook。他的兄弟姐妹，11 岁的夏洛特公主和 8 岁的路易斯王子，也都在这所学校上学，该学校位于伦敦西部温莎的家附近。

item.title: 七国集团 (G7) 上的热门麦克风捕捉到了世界领导人在重大话题上的谈话
item.description:
法国埃维昂莱班（美联社）——本周，世界上最富有的民主国家的领导人正在讨论如何解决世界上一些最大的问题，但七国集团峰会上的热麦克风显示，谈话还涉及更轻松的话题。运动的。香烟。天气。还有格陵兰岛的事吗？
当世界各国领导人走进湖畔度假胜地的会议室时，为他们有关战争和贸易的重要讨论而设置的麦克风经常会出现即兴的玩笑。
特朗普在最后一天的谈判中大步走进来，并宣称“我是老板！”他的同行们围坐在一张大型椭圆形会议桌旁，等待开始讨论有关中国正在向出口市场大量提供补贴产品的担忧。领导们都笑了。
周二，意大利总理乔治亚·梅洛尼的吸烟习惯成为热门话题。当德国总理弗里德里希·梅尔齐夫问她那天早上是否已经抽烟时，梅洛尼透露，她“自五月一号以来”就没有吸烟了。
2026 年 6 月 16 日星期二，意大利总理乔治亚·梅洛尼 (Giorgia Meloni) 在法国埃维昂莱班 (Evian-les-Bains) 出席与七国集团和中东领导人举行的工作午餐会。（Evelyn Hockstein/Pool Photo via AP）
她对烟草的反对引起了加拿大、英国、日本和欧盟领导人的热烈祝贺。梅洛尼举起双手庆祝。与此同时，加拿大总理马克·卡尼向她提出了一个问题。
“你有补丁吗？”卡尼抓住自己的手臂问道。
随着世界杯在美国、墨西哥和加拿大举行，足球自然成为讨论的焦点。
周二，当各国领导人齐聚一堂共进午餐时，法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙和其他人也发表了看法。有人高喊“Allez les bleus”，这是为法国队加油的声音。可以听到另一位领导人谈论巴黎圣日耳曼最近取得的冠军联赛胜利。
2026 年 6 月 16 日星期二，法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙站在唐纳德·特朗普总统（左）和加拿大总理马克·卡尼（中）旁边，与七国集团和中东领导人在法国埃维昂莱班共进工作午餐。（克里斯蒂安·哈特曼 / 美联社泳池照片）
美国总统唐纳德·特朗普将注意力转向周日在白宫举办的 UFC 笼式比赛。特朗普在 80 岁生日时坐在场边，热情洋溢地谈到了 UFC 首席执行官达纳·怀特 (Dana White)。
周二，英国首相凯尔·斯塔米尔对佛得角出人意料地0-0战平世界杯冠军西班牙队表示否认。“我不得不说，非常了不起，”他说。
在一个有趣的时刻，特朗普被拍到与欧洲理事会主席安东尼奥·科斯塔通过麦克风交谈。
2026 年 6 月 16 日星期二，唐纳德·特朗普总统在法国埃维昂莱班与七国集团和中东领导人共进工作午餐时，聆听欧洲理事会主席安东尼奥·科斯塔（左）的讲话。（克里斯蒂安·哈特曼 / 美联社泳池照片）
“你明白吗？”特朗普说道，然后停下来，直视科斯塔。“格陵兰。”谈话的开始和结束尚不清楚。
欧洲政界人士对特朗普威胁收购丹麦半自治领土格陵兰岛感到愤怒。
周二，马克龙在离开该组织的工作午餐时似乎忘记了手表，特朗普对此增添了一些轻松的气氛。卡尼提请注意，说道：“他把手表落在这儿了。我们找到了他的手表。”
“如果他走了，给我，给我，”特朗普插话道，引来全场笑声。
有一些送礼外交的例子。
国际自行车联盟主席大卫·拉帕蒂恩在社交媒体上表示，马克龙为所有七位同行提供了个性化自行车，以宣传明年在法国阿尔卑斯山举行的自行车世界锦标赛。
没有任何热门麦克风时刻来检测特朗普的反应，他不喜欢骑自行车，并开玩笑说除了常规的高尔夫郊游外，他还进行了极少的锻炼。
梅尔茨最近因伊朗战争问题与特朗普发生争执，他向特朗普赠送了一件印有特朗普名字和号码47的德国国家队球衣。特朗普举起球衣微笑着合影，然后将其放在一边。
2026 年 6 月 16 日星期二，在法国埃维昂莱班举行的七国集团峰会工作会议上，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普（右）举起印有 47 号的球衣，英国首相基尔·斯塔默（左）和德国总理弗里德里希·梅尔茨在场。（美联社照片/Thibault Camus，Pool）
梅尔茨在社交媒体上发布了一张交流照片，并传达了一条尖锐的信息：“毕竟，我们是同一个团队的。”
宾克利从华盛顿报道，苏佩维尔从日内瓦报道。美联社驻布鲁塞尔撰稿人山姆·麦克尼尔对此报道做出了贡献。

item.title: 巴西最高法院以胁迫罪判处前总统博尔索纳罗之子有罪
item.description:
圣保罗（美联社）——巴西最高法院周二判定前议员爱德华多·博尔索纳罗（Eduardo Bolsonaro）因与去年因政变未遂罪判处其父亲、前总统雅伊尔·博尔索纳罗（Jair Bolsonaro）27年监禁的审判有关的胁迫罪有罪。
法院判处其有期徒刑四年零两个月。审理此案的五名法官都同意，他游说美国政府威胁巴西官员停止审判，这是非法干涉。
负责监督前总统政变未遂案的法官亚历山大·德莫赖斯表示，爱德华多·博尔索纳罗作为联邦立法者的工作“不是游说海外反对他自己的国家”。德莫赖斯和他的妻子去年7月受到美国政府的制裁。
爱德华多·博尔索纳罗的律师对判决提出异议，称没有足够的证据对他定罪。这位前议员自 2025 年 2 月以来一直居住在德克萨斯州。
美国总统唐纳德·特朗普去年对巴西征收 50% 的关税，以抗议雅伊尔·博尔索纳罗因试图推翻他在 2022 年选举中输给总统路易斯·伊纳西奥·卢拉·达席尔瓦而受到的起诉。
特朗普与卢拉的关系似乎在5月初巴西领导人访问白宫时有所改善，但随后在6月，美国政府再次提议对巴西进口商品征收25%的关税，声称世界第十大经济体从事不合理的贸易行为。
卢拉表示，5月初访问华盛顿期间，他向特朗普递交了显示美国对巴西存在贸易顺差的文件。
爱德华多·博尔索纳罗没有对最高法院的裁决发表评论。他正在为他的兄弟森竞选。弗拉维奥·博尔索纳罗（Flávio Bolsonaro）有望在 10 月份的选举中挑战卢拉，尽管他的候选资格最近面临与向一名名誉扫地的银行家付款有关的丑闻。
爱德华多·博尔索纳罗和弗拉维奥·博尔索纳罗最近在华盛顿拜访了包括特朗普在内的美国官员。
关注美联社对拉丁美洲的报道：https://apnews.com/hub/latin-america

item.title: 法官维持前威斯康星州法官汉娜·杜根 (Hannah Dugan) 帮助移民逃避 ICE 的定罪
item.description:
威斯康星州麦迪逊（美联社）——周二，一名联邦法官拒绝推翻威斯康星州法官对一名男子妨碍司法公正的定罪，因为移民官员出现在法庭上试图拘留他。
汉娜·杜根 (Hannah Dugan) 在被定罪后从密尔沃基县巡回法院辞职，该案是对法院如何应对唐纳德·特朗普总统全面移民镇压的早期考验。
特朗普的盟友将杜根称为激进法官，而她的支持者则表示她受到了不公平的攻击。
美国地区法官林恩·阿德尔曼 (Lynn Adelman) 于 6 月 3 日推迟了对杜根的判决，以考虑是否应该推翻对她的定罪。但阿德尔曼在周二的裁决中表示，杜根的定罪将维持不变。他没有立即设定宣判日期。
杜根的法律辩护团队在一份声明中表示：“法院的判决是错误的。”
杜根的律师辩称，她帮助爱德华多·弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯离开法院的定罪是无效的，应该被推翻。他说这是必要的，因为联邦上诉法院在四月份推翻了法官和检察官在杜根案件中引用的弗吉尼亚州关键移民案件。
在弗吉尼亚州的案件中，一名非法居留该国的移民被美国移民和海关执法人员拘留，后来逃脱。他被重新抓获并被指控妨碍未决的移民程序。
联邦上诉法院认为，ICE 的行动并不构成联邦妨碍司法法所要求的“待决程序”。
杜根的律师辩称，她不应该受到指控，因为 ICE 特工在她的法庭上没有针对这名移民的“待决诉讼”，只是发出了逮捕令。杜根的律师辩称，根据法律，提交搜查令并不构成“诉讼”。
检察官反驳说，弗吉尼亚州案件的事实有所不同，不适用于杜根的案件。他们还辩称，其他案件也支持杜根的定罪。
阿德尔曼表示，试图逮捕弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯确实算作“待决程序”，部分原因是这是一次有计划和有针对性的行动，而不是随机遭遇的逮捕。
“被告辩称，ICE 在这里充当执法机构，”阿德尔曼写道。“但这忽略了一个事实，即与联邦调查局等机构不同，ICE 可以发出自己的逮捕令、裁决并实施驱逐，就像对弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯所做的那样，无需法院参与。这很重要。”
12 月 19 日，陪审团判定 67 岁的杜根有罪，她将面临最高五年的监禁，但她不太可能被判处监禁。联邦量刑指南通常要求对像她这样没有犯罪历史且被判犯有非暴力犯罪的被告缓刑。
杜根在被定罪两周后，因共和党州议员威胁弹劾，辞去了密尔沃基县巡回法官的职务。她当法官已经九年了。
特朗普政府在总统继续推行全面的移民镇压之际对杜根提起诉讼。特朗普政府及其盟友将杜根称为一名激进法官，而杜根的律师则表示她受到了不公平的攻击，并辩称她因是法官而免于受到指控，但没有成功。
杜根的案件标志着威斯康星州法官首次因妨碍移民代理的指控而接受审判。她因隐匿他人以防止被捕的轻罪而被判无罪。
2025 年 4 月 18 日，移民官员在得知弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯非法重新进入该国后前往密尔沃基县法院，并计划在杜根面前出席州殴打案听证会。
杜根在法庭外与特工对峙，并指示他们前往首席法官办公室，因为她告诉他们，他们的行政逮捕令不足以逮捕弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯。
特工离开后，她带领弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯和他的律师走出私人陪审团大门。特工在走廊里发现了弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯，跟踪他到外面，并在一番追逐后逮捕了他。一周后，联邦调查局特工在法院逮捕了杜根，并给她戴上手铐。

item.title: B-52 在空中飞行了很短的时间后就坠毁并导致机上 8 人全部遇难
item.description:
LOS ANGELES (AP) — TheB-52 that crashedduring a test flight atEdwards Air Force Base in Californiawas in the air a very short time before slamming into the ground about halfway down the runway.
All eight people aboard were killed in Monday’s fiery crash of theBoeingB-52 Stratofortress, which was taking part in a test mission as part of a program to keep the oldest aircraft in the U.S. fleet flying for decades to come.
No cause has been determined. Officials at the base said it could take six months to complete the investigation.
The bomber took off shortly before noon on a clear day, heading southwest into the prevailing winds. It flew straight and crashed on the same 15,000-foot (4,572-meter) runway. The compact wreckage indicates the plane dropped sharply.
Officials have not yet released the names of the crash victims.
Lauren Smith told Eyewitness News KBAK-CBS and FOX58 that her husband, Jeromy Smith, was among the victims. He was a flight test engineer for the U.S. Department of Defense and died doing what he loved, she said.
“It is such a horrible hurt, and I’m still processing everything that happened,” she said.
The airfield remained closed Tuesday. Crews were making the crash site safe for search and recovery teams to enter, after fires flared up overnight, said Mike Paoli, a spokesperson for the 412 Test Wing at Edwards.
The aircraft was supporting a “radar modernization program,” Col. James Hayes, the deputy commander for the 412 Test Wing, said Monday. In 2025, Boeing sent a B-52 to Edwards with a modernized radar system that is key to keeping the bomber in the air through at least 2050, nearly a century after it first entered service.
At a news conference Monday, the Deputy Commander for Edwards Air Force Base, Col. James Hayes, said the cause of the crash is unknown and is being investigated.
A test team planned to conduct ground and flight test activities on the aircraft throughout 2026 to feed a production decision, the Air Force said in a 2025 news release. The modern Active Electronically Scanned Array (AESA) radar system replaced the aircraft’s antiquated radar. It was unclear if that was the same aircraft involved in Monday’s crash.
AESA replaced 1960s radar technology and offers improved navigation and targeting capabilities, according to a 2023 news release from Raytheon, which designed the new system for the Air Force’s entire B-52 fleet.
The B-52, a long-range bomber that entered service in 1955, is designed to carry both conventional and nuclear weapons. It has beenused in conflicts involving the U.S. military from Vietnamto Iran.
Along with a new radar, the fleet of 76 B-52s are scheduled to receive additional upgrades, including new engines, crew compartments, conventional and nuclear communication systems, avionics and weapons. The military said the goal is to make the B-52 a complement to the Air Force’s newest strategic bomber, theB-21 Raider.
Aerial footage showed virtually nothing left of the aircraft that went down at the base in the Mojave Desert about 100 miles (161 km) northeast of Los Angeles. Officials determined no one could have survived after reviewing footage of the crash, Hayes said at a news conference.
Those on the B-52 included government contractors, Boeing employees and uniformed military.
Edwards is home to the 412th Test Wing, which conducts regular developmental testing of all Air Force aircraft, weapons systems, software and components before purchase by the service as well as throughout their life span. Test missions take place at Edwards daily, Hayes said.
The base is whereAir Force test pilot Chuck Yeagerreached a speed of Mach 1.05 and broke the sound barrier in 1947.
Aviation safety experts have said their first thoughts about what might have caused the crash were about a malfunction in the flight controls or engines, but it is way too early to know. And investigators will consider a myriad of factors, including the age and maintenance of the plane.
J. Joseph, a retired Marine Corps colonel and airline pilot. said that even in a B-52 with eight engines, a malfunction can make the plane difficult to control if the pilot loses the outboard engines, and the forces pushing the plane get out of balance in a condition Joseph called asymmetric thrust. Although if there is time, the pilots can adjust the other throttles to rebalance the forces.
Heather Penney, a former F-16 combat pilot and aviation expert, said she knew one of the people who died aboard the B-52 personally — reinforcing how tragic this crash is for the close-knit community of military aviators. She declined to name the person before officials do.
She said it is unlikely that pilot error caused this crash given the expert training and experience of the test pilots on this flight. The age of the B-52 also opens up the possibility of problems with the structure of the plane.
“The youngest B- 52 was delivered to the Air Force in 1962. That was before the Cuban missile crisis, before the first man walked on the moon, before we had personal computers,” said Penney, who is director of Studies and Research at The Mitchell Institute for Aerospace Studies. “These are old airplanes. They’re structurally robust, but they are old aircraft. So structural failure can’t be ruled out.”
All the modernization efforts and upgrades that have been made to the B-52s over the decades have extended the life of these planes. At some point, these bombers will have to be replaced, but for now they continue to play a crucial role for the Air Force.
“The B-52 fleet that we have today, is the backbone of America’s bomber force. It’s over 50% of our bomber force, and it can go further, have larger payload, and stay airborne longer without refueling than any of our other bombers,” Penney said. “There’s no other bomber in our force has the attributes of the B-52. It’s been a workhorse. It’s going to continue to be a workhorse.”
This story has been updated to correct details of the bomber’s flight based on data analysis by AirNav Systems. AirNav now says the plane took off toward the southwest, not the northeast flew straight and crashed almost immediately, and was not airborne for 3 minutes and did not make a turn. It flew straight and crashed almost immediately.
Funk reported from Omaha, Nebraska. Associated Press journalist Konstantin Toropin contributed from Washington, D.C.

item.title: 在法院发生性关系并参加政治活动的法官同意退出佐治亚州选举案
item.description:
亚特兰大（美联社）——一名联邦法官在调查发现她在自己的办公室里与一名警察发生性关系并参加了一场党派活动，然后在面对指控时撒了谎，受到纪律处分。在美国司法部对她的公正能力提出质疑后，她在一场关于佐治亚州选举记录的斗争中回避了自己。
司法部试图将美国地区法官埃莉诺·罗斯从该案中除名，理由是据报道她参加了起诉唐纳德·特朗普总统的富尔顿县地方检察官法尼·威利斯的活动。罗斯周二提交了一份回避令，并写道，她这样做是“出于对潜在偏见的高度谨慎”。
司法部起诉佐治亚州国务卿布拉德·拉芬斯珀格，寻求一份未经编辑的全州选民名单，罗斯正在主持该案。
罗斯写道：“特朗普政府现在和威利斯过去的努力都变得严重两极分化。”她解释说，她“不能忽视”客观观察者可能会将她参加威利斯竞选活动赞助的活动解读为对地区检察官职位的支持，即使她只是去见了前同事。
法庭调查发现，罗斯在法院大楼内与一名身穿制服的高级警官在工作人员能听到的范围内发生性关系，参加了一场党派活动，然后最初撒谎否认了这些指控，随后罗斯受到了“私下谴责”。
调查报告称罗斯参加了地区检察官竞选活动主办的活动。法官表示，这位地区检察官自 1999 年以来一直是朋友，并承认曾前往活动期间举行的私人聚会，拜访地区检察官办公室的前同事。
罗斯此前曾在富尔顿县地方检察官办公室工作，并在威利斯担任地方检察官之前与威利斯有重叠的工作。
威利斯于 2023 年 8 月获得了针对特朗普和其他 18 人的起诉书，指控他们参与了一项旨在推翻佐治亚州 2020 年选举结果的广泛计划。该案最终于 11 月被驳回。

item.title: 白宫谈话要点声称在最初的伊朗协议中取得了胜利，但往往不符合现实
item.description:
WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House has informed supporters that President Donald Trump has accomplished his goals in the war with Iran despite the details of an initial agreementremaining unclearand negotiations on Tehran’s nuclear program still to be held.
In a series of talking points sent to Trump supporters and Republican members of Congress this week, the White House proclaimed major victories, such as Iran agreeing to never have a nuclear weapon, reopeningthe crucial Strait of Hormuzandfighting in Lebanonending.
The talking points, on White House letterhead, were obtained by The Associated Press from two recipients of the document and go against some of the realities on the ground, especially regarding whatIsrael has agreed toin its conflict with Hezbollah in Lebanon.
But the memorandum of understanding between the U.S. and Iran, expected to be signed Friday in Switzerland, is still a closely guarded secret, even among Republican allies in Congress and the Israelis. That has led to confusion,concern and skepticismamong all but the most hard-core Trump supporters about what has been agreed to.
Republicans acknowledged that the initial deal, by remaining under wraps, has created a vacuum that is being filled by potential misinformation.
“You don’t know what’s true and what’s not true — is it in there?” said Sen. Shelley Moore Capito, R-W.Va. “My speculation is that it’s probably still being written and fine-tuned, and the administration is not ready to release it until it’s all done.”
Asked why he was not releasing theterms of the initial agreement, Trump told reporters Tuesday at the Group of Seven summit in France that he would “like to get a formal setting first before we do that.”
“I’ll not only release it,” he went on to say, “I’ll probably have a press conference and read it to you word by word, so that the press covers it accurately.”
Trump said he was open to submitting an eventual agreement to Congress for review and approval.
“I like the idea, send it to Congress please,” Trump said. “I mean who wouldn’t approve it?”
Yet submitting a nuclear agreement with Iran to Congress is not optional under a law that was passed following the 2015 Iran nuclear agreement reached by then-President Barack Obama, which Trump abandoned during his first administration. Some congressional aides argue that even the presumed memorandum of understanding to be signed Friday would also be subject to lawmakers’ review.
The talking points claim that the Obama-era Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action, or JCPOA, was never signed, which is partly true but misleading. The foreign ministers who negotiated the agreement did sign a copy of the deal, although it was viewed as an informal document meant to memorialize the occasion.
More important, the JCPOA was endorsed and approved by the U.N. Security Council, which enshrined its provisions into international law.
“President Trump solved a threat Washington spent forty years managing,” according to the talking points. “Iran will never have a nuclear weapon.” Copies of the talking points were provided to the AP by a congressional aide and an outside government adviser.
Iran’s position dating back decades is that it has no desire to develop a nuclear weapon. Many Iran critics doubt that pledge because the country has 440.9 kilograms (972 pounds) of uranium that is enriched up to 60% purity, a short, technical step from weapons-grade levels of 90%, according tothe International Atomic Energy Agency.
Meanwhile, the talking points say “the Strait of Hormuz is open again, and energy prices American families pay every day are coming down.”
“American Families Are the Big Winners,” the document says. “Start with what this means at home. American families no longer have to fear a nuclear-armed Iran. They are going to feel relief at the pump and at the grocery store.”
The Strait of Hormuz, where a fifth of the world’s oil passed before the war began, had been open to all maritime traffic until Feb. 28 when Trump and Israel began attacking Iran. That means that an agreement to reopen the strait would start toreturn the situation to where it wason Feb. 27 before the U.S. and Israel spent billions of dollars to go to war. It could take weeks or even months for some normalcy to return.
Consumer prices in the United Statesand elsewhere only spiked after the war began and shipments of oil and other commodities through the strait were interrupted by Iran, which insists it will retain control of access to the crucial waterway no matter what.
The talking points say Iran will not receive any American taxpayer money for its eventual agreement with and adherence to an as-yet unnegotiated nuclear agreement and will only get financial incentives if it meets certain benchmarks.
They suggest that Obama’s 2015 nuclear accord cost U.S. taxpayers billions of dollars, when the monetary sanctions relief provided to Iran then actually came from frozen Iranian assets and not the U.S. treasury.
The talking points mention “the pallets of cash” the U.S. sent to Iran after the JCPOA was signed. In fact, the shipment of cash, which came from an Iranian payment for a canceled arms sale to the late Shah of Iran’s government, were unrelated to the nuclear deal.
That money was part of a swap that saw the release of several American citizens detained in Iran and of several Iranians imprisoned in the U.S.
The talking points trumpet Trump’s claim that the agreement will end the Israel-Hezbollah conflict in Lebanon.
“This signed agreement ends military operations on every front,” they say. “For the first time, that explicitly includes Lebanon, with a commitment to both Israel and Lebanon’s sovereignty and territorial integrity.”
However, Hezbollah is not party to talks that have been taking place in the U.S. between Israel and Lebanon, and the Iranian-backed militant group has rejected any agreements reached during them. Israeli officials also have said they will not be bound by the terms of the tentative Iran-U.S. agreement and do not know the details of it.
“We’re less encouraged about the fact that it seems that Lebanon has been included in the agreement with Iran,” Israeli Ambassador to the U.S. Yechiel Leiter told NPR. “And we think that that’s unnecessary and unhelpful.”
A senior U.S. official told reporters that Israel’s withdrawal from Lebanon was not a condition of the memorandum of understanding. The official spoke to reporters on condition of anonymity Monday to discuss outlines of the unreleased agreement.
Associated Press writers Aamer Madhani in Geneva, Darlene Superville in Evian-les-Bains, France, Koral Saeed in Jerusalem, and Michelle L. Price and Seung Min Kim in Washington contributed to this report.

item.title: 当局表示，他们扰乱了白宫 UFC 笼斗表演计划中的无人机和枪支袭击事件
item.description:
WASHINGTON (AP) — Law enforcement officials disrupted a planned attack targeting President Donald Trump’sUFC cage-fighting showat the White House this past weekend, according to court papers unsealed Tuesday that say plotters who harbored fringe conspiracy theories spoke of flying explosives-laden drones and shooting panicked crowd members as they fled.
Investigators recovered high-powered firearms from several of the suspects and reviewed encrypted text messages between roughly 20 participants who shared detailed maps and aerial photographs of the area and discussed the need for a “safe house” and escape routes after the intended attack, the documents show.
Diego Lopes celebrates during a featherweight bout against Steve Garcia during UFC Freedom 250 on the South Lawn of the White House, Sunday, June 14, 2026, in Washington. (AP Photo/Mark Schiefelbein)
But it’s unclear from the court records how close the would-be attackers could have come to being able to carry out the plan had it not been thwarted.
Several suspects or co-conspirators who were questioned by the authorities said they did not intend themselves to carry out violence but planned to instead observe others. One said he would have traveled to the UFC event as a protester but had to return home after his vehicle malfunctioned. And though the participants spoke of using drones rigged with explosives, charging documents suggest they were still looking to acquire such equipment when the plot was interrupted.
“It didn’t even get close to the point of execution,” Vice President JD Vance said Tuesday evening on Fox News Channel, describing the planning as “not that advanced.”
“They weren’t in town. They had not really done that much planning,” he said.
Law enforcement officials learned about the possible threat on June 10, four days beforethe mixed martial arts extravaganzaon the White House’s South Lawn, “and thanks to the rapid action of the FBI, our partners, and the Department of Justice in a multi-state operation, multiple individuals are now in custody and allegedly planned attacks were stopped cold,” Director Kash Patel said in a post on X on Tuesday.
AP correspondent Ed Donahue reports arrests have been made in connection with a planned attack on the White House.
Five people from states including Ohio, Missouri, Nebraska and California were arrested on federal charges, the Justice Department said.
Asked about the arrests Tuesday, Vance said there was “more violent rhetoric coming from the left than the right these days.” But the charging documents paint a more muddled view of their views, depicting them as espousing a tangled web of anti-government sentiment, antisemitic grievances, fury over the Trump administration’s handling of the Jeffrey Epstein files and conspiracy theories about a powerful elite that sacrifices and consumes children.
Both Trump and Vance said they had not been briefed in advance of the plot. A top Secret Service official suggested Tuesday the investigation was continuing and an announcement might have been premature.
“Anyone that believes that case was worked in a bubble is naive,” Deputy Secret Service Director Matthew Quinn told reporters at an unrelated news conference. “I’ll tell you the Secret Service led that investigation from the beginning. I’ll tell you that it’s ongoing. In order to maintain the integrity of the investigation and the security plan, we chose not to leak it.”
Among those arrested was Tycen Proper, a 19-year-old Ohio man whose mother contacted law enforcement last week with concerns about his firearms purchases and online communications, according to an FBI affidavit filed in the case.
Proper told officials he participated in the planning of an attack, according to the affidavit, which says some members of the group began communicating with each other last March through a TikTok group called “Vanguard of the Old.”
“The members of the group stated that they wanted to protect the United States, which they believed was headed in the wrong direction,” the affidavit says. “Members of the group believed that the United States needed to be torn down so that it could be rebuilt. Some expressed a desire that people who were involved withJeffrey Epsteinshould not govern the country.”
Security at the White House looks through a pair of binoculars during the UFC Fan Fest on the White House Ellipse ahead of Sunday’s fight on the South Lawn, June 13, 2026, in Washington. (AP Photo/Allison Robbert, File)
Trump, who celebrated his 80th birthday at the UFC event on Sunday, was friends with Epstein many years ago but has said he ended their relationship before the disgraced financier’s crimes became known. Epstein killed himself in a New York jail cell in 2019 as he awaited trial on sex trafficking charges.
President Donald Trump attends UFC Freedom 250 on the South Lawn of the White House, Sunday, June 14, 2026, in Washington. (AP Photo/Alex Brandon)
A lawyer for Proper, who is charged with firearms offenses and crimes including attempted murder of an officer or employee of the United States, did not immediately return a message seeking comment.
The logistics were discussed via Signal,an app that uses end-to-end encryptionfor its messaging and calling services, through a primary chat of “approximately 19 individuals” and smaller side chats, authorities said. Messages obtained from Proper’s phone show he identified by name several Republican lawmakers he said should be targeted because they apparently received donations from causes supportive of Israel, the affidavit said.
Proper told law enforcement officials that he had been planning to drive with weapons and body armor to a meet-up spot in Fredericksburg, Virginia, court papers say. He said though he did not intend to shoot people at the White House, others in the group did, the affidavit said.
The plan called for the use of drones that would be detonated over the north side of the White House, prompting an evacuation into the line of fire of waiting snipers in an attack Proper said was designed to “jumpstart” a revolution, authorities said.
Investigators who examined Proper’s phone and TikTok account identified additional suspects.
Michael Alan Thomas, 32, of Pinon Hills, California, told officials he viewed himself as “the planner and advisor for the group, and while he was not willing to take action himself, wanted to guide and instruct others on how to carry out attacks” designed to overthrow the government, an FBI agent said in an affidavit.
The agent said Thomas believed the U.S. government was “run by an elite group of individuals who sacrifice and consume infants who also were deeply involved” with Epstein and are now protected by Trump.
Another suspect, Bryan Omar Roa, also of California, told the FBI he had planned to attend the event as a “protester” but he had to return home because his car was broken, an agent said.
It was not immediately clear who their lawyers were.
Two other suspects were identified as Daniel K. Eskridge, 32, of Kidder, Missouri, who officials say said in a group chat that a target of the attack should be “big and someone a majority of the country knows,” and Abraham Hermosillo Alvarez, an Omaha, Nebraska, man who the FBI said posted detailed plans with the co-conspirators.
A lawyer for Alvarez declined to comment and a lawyer for Eskridge did not immediately return a message seeking comment.
Associated Press writers Darlene Superville in Évian-les-Bains, France, and Michael Kunzelman in Washington contributed to this report.

item.title: 特朗普在寻求与伊朗达成协议时追捕内塔尼亚胡，使他们的友谊面临考验
item.description:
以色列总理本杰明·内塔尼亚胡去年告诉唐纳德·特朗普总统，他是“以色列在白宫有史以来最伟大的朋友”。
现在，当特朗普试图敲定一项结束与伊朗战争的协议时，他向内塔尼亚胡发出了其他美国领导人都不敢公开使用的言论。
他声称以色列的存在归功于他——“没有我，就没有以色列”——并在采访中咒骂了他的判断。他甚至形容他“疯了”。
内塔尼亚胡担任总理的任期跨越了四位美国总统，他曾一度令所有美国总统感到沮丧。但没有人像特朗普那样公开表达这一点，他与内塔尼亚胡开始了冲突。
紧张局势发生之际，特朗普批评以色列最近对黎巴嫩的袭击，这威胁到华盛顿与德黑兰之间的谈判。特朗普一直在推动达成协议，因为他在国内面临政治反弹，这场战争在国内不受欢迎，并推高了汽油价格。
“如果内塔尼亚胡介入特朗普真正想要的事情，而且这不是这场战争，他就准备利用他所拥有的影响力，”二十年来担任民主党和共和党政府中东问题顾问的亚伦·戴维·米勒(Aaron David Miller)说。
协议定于周五在卢塞恩市附近的布尔根施托克度假村签署。特朗普周二在法国举行的年度七国集团峰会上发表讲话时表示，他告诉内塔尼亚胡，他对自己最近的举动感到不满。
“没有美国，就不会有以色列。没有我，就不会有以色列，因为没有其他总统愿意做我所做的事情，”特朗普说。“我和比比关系很好。现在比比必须对黎巴嫩承担更多责任。”
长期以来，华盛顿两党就支持以色列达成了共识，但近年来这种共识已经出现了分歧。自由派对以色列对待巴勒斯坦人的方式越来越感到愤怒，尤其是在加沙战争期间，而保守派则质疑美国长期支持以色列的重要性。左翼和右翼都存在对反犹太主义的担忧。
特朗普的最新言论迅速招致左翼团体的批评。
“他将以色列的存在视为取决于他，”美国犹太民主委员会主席海莉·索伊弗 (Halie Soifer) 说。“这对绝大多数关心以色列未来的犹太人来说是一种极大的冒犯。”
总统乔·拜登和副总统卡马拉·哈里斯在加沙战争期间经常与内塔尼亚胡意见不合，有时甚至公开批评内塔尼亚胡。但他们更加谨慎，以避免面临反以色列的指控。
2025 年 10 月 13 日，唐纳德·特朗普总统在特拉维夫附近的本古里安国际机场登上空军一号之前，与以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡合影留念，以色列总统艾萨克·赫尔佐格在左侧观看。（美联社照片/Evan Vucci，档案）
对于特朗普公开谴责内塔尼亚胡的严重性，保守派、亲以色列团体存在分歧。
共和党犹太联盟主席马特·布鲁克斯将特朗普的批评描述为家庭成员之间不可避免的分歧。
布鲁克斯驳斥称，他的政党对特朗普言论的任何无声批评都代表着一个复杂的政治信息，因为特朗普一直可靠地支持以色列担任总统。
布鲁克斯说：“如果拜登或哈里斯说了一些批评的话，那是因为他们对以色列怀有敌意，或者对以色列的支持程度不如特朗普总统。”
他指出了特朗普第一届政府在将美国驻以色列大使馆从特拉维夫迁至耶路撒冷以及在总统第二任期内从加沙返回以色列人质等方面所发挥的作用。
拜登曾批评内塔尼亚胡对加沙战争的处理方式，尽管特朗普对内塔尼亚胡的批评伴随着“在这个问题上的巨大善意储备，这是拜登和哈里斯从未有过的”。
亲以色列倡导者莫特·克莱因表示，特朗普应该将这些言论保密，特别是考虑到他多年来对土耳其、朝鲜和中国独裁领导人的公开赞扬。
保守的美国犹太复国主义组织主席克莱因表示，他担心特朗普公开发表这些言论是为了吸引以色列批评者，“因为他看到美国人对以色列变得比以往任何时候都更加敌视。”

item.title: 梅西上演帽子戏法追平男子世界杯进球纪录，阿根廷队击败阿尔及利亚队
item.description:
KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Lionel Messi used the front of his white-and-blue, sweat-soaked jersey to wipe the tears from his eyes, a flood of emotions cracking his usually calm, confident demeanor after he gave Argentina an early lead inits World Cup openeragainst Algeria.
Suddenly, any questions about Messi’s hamstring injury, or whether he could help Argentina become the third team to win consecutive World Cups — even as his 39th birthday approaches next week — had been answered. Witha brilliant hat trick in a 3-0 winover Les Fennecs, Messi moved into a tie with Germany’s Miroslav Klose for the career scoring record at the men’s World Cup.
“My tears after the first goal? I’ve had some tough days. It wasn’t related to football. And those feelings were because of that,” Messi said afterward, without elaborating. “I thank my teammates, the coaching staff and the delegation for helping me.”
Messiscored that emotional first goalin the opening minutes on a nifty feed from Inter Miami teammate Rodrigo De Paul,the secondoff an opportunistic rebound early in the second half, andthe thirdon a crisp strike moments before subbing out to a standing ovation from a crowd of 69,045 tilted heavily toward the three-time World Cup champions.
“At a loss for words about Leo. What can I say?” Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni said. “He’s incredible.”
His incredible trio of goals came 20 years to the day that Messi made hisWorld Cupdebut in a match against Serbia and Montenegro — he scored in that one, too — and made the pride of Rosario only the second player to score in five editions of the men’s tournament.
Argentina’s Lionel Messi reacts after scoring his third goal during the World Cup Group J soccer match between Argentina and Algeria in Kansas City, Mo., Tuesday, June 16, 2026. (AP Photo/Charlie Riedel)
Messi has 16 goals in his record six World Cup appearances overall, and it seems inevitable that Klose’s record will fall in the coming weeks. The hat trick was the 61st of Messi’s career, his 11th while playing in his national team colors and his first in the World Cup.
It also was the fifth straight World Cup game in which Messi has scored.
“It makes me very happy to have lived through everything that came my way. What I’m living though now is the cherry on top,” Messi said. “I’m very happy an grateful for this wonderful group. I enjoy it so much.”
Messi upstaged two of soccer’s other stars — Kylian Mbappé of France and Erling Haaland of Norway — who had big games of their own on Tuesday. Mbappéscored twice in France’s 3-1 winover Senegal to move into a tie for fourth on the men’s World Cup goals list with 14, while Haaland scored twice for Norway in its 4-1 victory over Iraq.
“Messi is a madman,” Haaland said in a post on Snapchat during Argentina’s game.
Messi had been dealing with a minor hamstring injury with Inter Miami that slowed him in the lead-up to the World Cup. But the eight-time winner of the Ballon d’Or, which honors global soccer’s best player, had no problems in a tuneup last week with Iceland, scoring on a penalty kick while playing 20 minutes in a sharp performance.
Argentina’s Lionel Messi (10) shoots and scores their third goal against Algeria’s Riyad Mahrez (7) and Nabil Bentaleb (19) during the World Cup Group J soccer match between Argentina and Algeria in Kansas City, Mo., Tuesday, June 16, 2026. (AP Photo/Reed Hoffmann)
“This is my sixth World Cup, and I still feel like I’m in good shape,” Messi said. “Fortunately, I’m doing well, and today we managed to win a tough match. It’s important to start the tournament with a victory in the first game, as that’s never easy in a World Cup.”
Messi’s appearance against Algeria was the 200th of his international career, which began in 2005 at age of 18. The only players with more are Portugal’s Cristiano Ronaldo, who will play his 229th on Wednesday, and Bader al-Mutawa, who played in 202 for Kuwait.
Messi and Ronaldo are the only men to have scored in five World Cups.
“Class is permanent,” Algeria coach Vladimir Petkovic said. “He’s fortunate to have the privilege that the entire Argentina team works for him, and supports him, and for a number of years now — decades — he’s done incredible things.”
Argentina is among four national teams making their base camps in the Kansas City metro. And much as it has the rest of the world, Messi-mania has swept through the area ever since La Albiceleste’s arrival in the Heartland about two weeks ago.
On match day,thousands of fanswearing his No. 10 jersey trekked into the home of the NFL’s Chiefs on the outskirts of Kansas City, singing odes to their hero. Meanwhile, during a watch party at the downtown Power & Light District, a goat accompanied by former NFL quarterback-turned Fox broadcaster Jameis Winston came on stage wearing an Argentina jersey.
The humorous moment seemed to have foreshadowed a big night for Messi when he scored an hour later, and the argument that he’s soccer’s GOAT — the greatest of all time — is becoming no argument at all with every match he plays.
“It’s an advantage to have Leo because of how he handles the group and pushes it forward. Because of who he is,” De Paul said. “He doesn’t care about individual records. He prioritizes the group, and for us it’s incredible.”
AP World Cup:https://apnews.com/fifa-world-cup

item.title: 巨人队投手在骄傲之夜添加圣经经文后，美国职业棒球大联盟警告球员不要改变制服
item.description:
旧金山（美联社）——上周，旧金山先发球员兰登·鲁普和另外两名投手在他们的骄傲睡帽上添加了圣经经文，美国职业棒球大联盟警告球员不要在制服上写字。
周五晚上，鲁普以 5-1 输给芝加哥小熊队，他的帽子上写着“Gen 9:12-16”。圣经经文的一部分与球员们在骄傲之夜佩戴的彩虹 SF 标志重叠。旧金山中继投手 JT Brubaker 和 Ryan Walker 也在他们的帽子上添加了圣经经文。
另一位巨人队投手、左投手萨姆·亨特格斯则戴着球队标准的带有橙色标志的黑色帽子，而不是骄傲之夜版本。
美国职棒大联盟对未来违反联盟制服政策的行为发出警告。
美国职棒大联盟在最初的声明中表示：“帽子上的文字违反了我们的规则，按照正常做法，我们已经警告球员未来的违规行为。”
联盟周二发布了另一份声明进行详细说明。
美国职业棒球大联盟表示：“需要明确的是，这种例行的口头警告不要在未来的比赛中戴帽子并不是纪律，也与信息的内容完全无关。”
“我们尊重球员自由表达的权利。但是，根据美国职业棒球大联盟的《统一规定》，禁止以任何形式书写任何信息，该规定部分规定，‘(a) 球员不得在服装或比赛设备上书写、附加、贴上、刺绣或以其他方式显示昵称或信息……’。我们过去曾多次就“爸爸”、“母亲节快乐、我爱妈妈”和家庭成员姓名等信息向球员发出同样的警告。
MLB 球队有时会在常规赛期间举办骄傲之夜来支持他们的 LGBTQ+ 粉丝。该联盟在举办骄傲之夜方面一直是美国四大职业体育运动中的佼佼者，部分原因是其常规赛与六月的骄傲月重叠。许多人采用彩虹色的制服补丁或标志，在球场周围设置特殊标牌，并邀请社区领袖和变装表演者等嘉宾。
鲁普在他的帽子上引用的《创世记》经文的一部分包括上帝承诺不再在全世界范围内引发洪水，而天空中的彩虹就是该圣约的标志。
鲁普对记者说：“这就是我所相信的事情，我对此坚定不移，我很庆幸我们生活在一个国家，我们可以自由地相信我们想要的东西……并表达我们想要的东西。”
巨人队主教练托尼·维特洛告诉记者，赛前没有与投手讨论任何事情。维泰洛表示，球员“可以自由地做他们认为最好的事情”，这“只是一种常识”。

item.title: 前 NHL 球员凯尔·卡尔德因病去世，享年 47 岁
item.description:
前 NHL 球员凯尔·卡尔德周一去世。他47岁了。
他的女儿麦迪逊在社交媒体帖子中宣布了父亲的去世消息。考尔德在 2020-22 赛季执教的洛杉矶小国王队表示，考尔德在短暂患病后去世。
麦迪逊·考尔德在 Instagram 上写道：“在一百万年内我都不会想到这一天会到来。”“我的心里永远有一个空虚的地方，但永远有一个属于你的地方。”
1999年至2009年间，考尔德作为边锋在联盟中打了608场常规赛和季后赛。这位土生土长的艾伯塔省曼维尔人大部分时间都在芝加哥度过，也曾为费城、底特律、洛杉矶和阿纳海姆效力。
“凯尔体现了我们校友家庭如此特别的价值观：他的忠诚、坚韧、慷慨以及对周围人坚定不移的承诺，”NHL 校友会在周二的纪念他的帖子中表示。“他像冰上的钉子一样坚强，是一个永不退缩的激烈竞争者，但在勇气的背后却有一颗泰迪熊的心。凯尔非常关心他的队友、朋友和每个有幸认识他的人。他是一个保护者，一个忠诚的朋友，一个总是把别人放在自己之上的人。”
自 2018 年以来，考尔德在芝加哥、洛杉矶和波士顿执教青年队。
黑鹰队董事长兼首席执行官丹尼·沃茨 (Danny Wirtz) 表示：“凯尔以与他的比赛相同的热情、强度和令人难以置信的奉献精神对待生活。”“在他的职业生涯结束后很长一段时间内，他对这项运动的承诺仍然坚定，与各代球员分享他对曲棍球的热爱。”

item.title: 汤姆·赫兰德似乎证实他和赞达亚已经结婚
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——汤姆·霍兰德显然已经证实，他和他的多年爱人赞达亚已经喜结连理。
这位演员可能结束了赞达亚的造型师劳·罗奇 (Law Roach) 三月份声称这对夫妇已经举行婚礼而引发的数月猜测。在 2026 年的一个转折中，人工智能在揭秘中发挥了作用。
当《Esquire U.K.》问霍兰德是否必须告知家人有关他和赞达亚在婚礼上出现的人工智能图像时，霍兰德回答说：“不需要，因为他们都在那里。”
“这就是你所能得到的一切，”他谈到此事时说道。
在周二发表的报道中，霍兰德滔滔不绝地讲述了这对夫妇如何在镜头后相互支持。
他说：“我们的业务可能会面临压力很大的情况，拥有经得起时间考验的关系基石真是太好了。”
“所以，对我来说，我找到了我的人。她是我最好的朋友，和她在一起时我是最快乐的，但我也从未感到如此支持和安全。就这样。”
霍兰德和赞达亚第一次见面是在 2017 年共同主演电影《蜘蛛侠：英雄归来》时。今年夏天，他们一起主演了大片：《奥德赛》和《蜘蛛侠：崭新的一天》。

item.title: “痴迷”是一种感觉。每个人，包括库里·巴克，都在试图弄清楚这意味着什么
item.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Days before“Obsession” opened in theaters, its 26-year-old director, Curry Barker, made a bet with his manager and agent. They said if the movie opened above $20 million, they would all get tattoos.
“Obsession” fell just short. It debuted with $17 million. They were still thrilled. Barker made the horror film with just $750,000. It was enormously successful. But then something unexpected happened. The following weekend, “Obsession”easily cleared $20 million.And then it did again and again and almost a fourth time — an almost unheard-of staying power.
“It was just like: Holy cow. I didn’t think that was an option,” Barker says. “Now we’ve said if it hits $300 million, we’ll all get the tattoo. We had to make a new milestone. And I think we’ll reach it.”
This image released by Focus Features shows director Curry Barker on the set of “Obsession.” (Manny Liotta/Focus Features via AP)
Over the last month, “Obsession” has sent shock waves through Hollywood. Barker’s microbudget thriller has grossed $286 million worldwide, and it’s still going. On its fifth weekend in theaters, it wassecond only to Steven Spielberg’s “Disclosure Day,”with $19 million. In North America, it has outgrossed “Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu.” It’s the biggest hit in the 24-year existence of Focus Features, which has had to postpone the video-on-demand release. It ranks among the most profitable movies ever made.
Barker, who built a following making sketches and short films on YouTube, is living out the dream of every aspiring filmmaker. Life, he granted in a recent interview, is different now.
“My day to day is pretty much the same. It’s just that when I go out in public, it’s a lot different,” he says, laughing. “I actually feel a little unsafe sometimes.”
That’s an ironic development for someone whose twist on an old Monkey Paw story has frightened moviegoers. In “Obsession,” Bear Bailey (Michael Johnston) wishes on an antique toy called a One Wish Willow that his crush, Nikki (Inde Navarrette), loved him. The spell — loosely inspired byan old “Simpsons” Halloween episode— works disturbingly well.
This image released by Focus Features shows Inde Navarrette, left, and Michael Johnston in a scene from “Obsession.” (Focus Features via AP)
The astonishing success of “Obsession” has been hotly debated throughout the industry. Coupled withthe A24 hit “Backrooms,”by 20-year-old Kane Parsons, it’s been a coming-out party for YouTube as a breeding ground for the next generation of filmmakers.
It’s also brought waves of Gen Z moviegoers — who already make up a promisingly robust percentage of frequent ticket buyers — into theaters. Summer has historically been dominated by legacy franchises, but “Obsession” may represent a sea change.
“If there’s a lesson from ‘Obsession,’ I think it’s about audiences,” says Peter Kujawski, chairman of Focus Features. “We have a generation that grew up online, approaches culture with enormous curiosity and playfulness, and is far less concerned with where a filmmaker comes from than whether the story connects. They’re engaged, incredibly film-literate and eager to champion new voices and original stories.”
Barker, who grew up in Mobile, Alabama, before moving to Los Angeles at 18, says he feels as though he’s writing for his generation. The response to “Obsession,” he says, taps into a collective need.
“I get it because I think we’re a little tired of being at home. Our generation is the COVID generation,” says Barker. “I was fortunate enough to have all four years of high school experience. My brother, Riley, lost two years of that. We’re sick of the phones.”
Barker wanted to be an actor before he wanted to be a filmmaker. And while his early exposure to “The Texas Chainsaw Massacre,” at age 11, helped set him on a horror path, he didn’t begin that way.
“I was a huge Harry Potter fan growing up. Huge. I was obsessed,” Barker says, smiling. “I had all the wands. I would dress up.”
This image released by Focus Features shows Inde Navarrette and Michael Johnston in a scene from “Obsession.” (Focus Features via AP)
Barker attended film school in Los Angeles for a year, where he met Cooper Tomlinson, a co-star and producer on “Obsession.” The two soon forged their own path, though, on YouTube and TikTok. Their comedy sketch series, “That’s a Bad Idea,” found a footing online.
Barker wrote and directed the 2023 short “The Chair,” which attracted the interest of Tea Shop Productions. Producer James Harris approached Barker about a feature of “The Chair,” but he instead wanted to make a film — “Obsession” — that drew on many of the same ideas. Meanwhile, Barker also made an $800 found-footage horror film, “Milk & Serial.” After failing to secure distribution, he simply uploaded to YouTube. It went viral and landed him an agent.
“Obsession” was selected to premiere at the Toronto International Film Festival last year, giving it an enviable platform. After a bidding war, Kujawski and Focus acquired it for $15 million.
“What stands out about Curry is that he isn’t working from an inherited playbook,” says Kujawski. “Whether you look at his earlier work or ‘Obsession,’ there’s a consistency of vision and a confidence in his storytelling that immediately sets him apart. He knows exactly what he wants to say while being absolutely committed to making every minute of his work as entertaining as possible, and he’s willing to take real risks in service of that vision.”
Barker’s swift but hard-earned rise has made him the poster boy for a new brand of filmmaker, one who has honed his craft as a digital creator and arrives with an established fan base. Jason Blum, the chief executive of Blumhouse Productions, has compared Barker and company to the 1970s wave of American auteurs, “making edgy movies that are connecting in theaters in a crazy way.”
“When you really step back, my journey is not really that different than Christopher Nolan or David Fincher or Steven Spielberg,” Barker says. “You can watch their early short films and see their work before they were given a chance. I think YouTube is just a path, a platform we can use now to show the industry what we’ve got.”
Now, Barker is one of the most in-demand filmmakers in Hollywood. He has already shot his next feature, “Anything But Ghosts,” starring Aaron Paul and Bryce Dallas Howard, for Blumhouse. Two months ago, A24 announced that he will write and direct a reboot of “The Texas Chainsaw Massacre.”
This image released by Focus Features shows Inde Navarrette in a scene from “Obsession.” (Focus Features via AP)
All the attention has taken some getting used to. Filmmakers like Ari Aster and Zach Cregger and even Spielberg have reached out to compliment Barker on his film.
“That’s when you start to feel this impostor syndrome of like: What? It’s not that good,” Barker says, laughing. “All I see when I watch ‘Obsession’ is the problems.”
An “Obsession” sequel is, naturally, a certainty. “A sequel isn’t hard for this movie,” grants Barker. He sketches out how new wishes by other characters on One Wish Willows could lead to entirely different stories, all revolving around some new vice: greed, fame, whatever.
But as much as it’s tempting to see “Obsession” as the product of Barker’s own wish, it’s more like the opposite. In the film, Bear’s profound mistake is putting off confessing his feelings to Nikki, thinking there’s plenty of time to do it. (The movie immediately cuts to a dead cat.) Barker, on the other hand, had no timidity about realizing his dreams. He wanted to make movies, so he did.
“Anyone that asks what advice you have for young filmmakers, I always say the same thing,” says Barker. “I went to a film school for a year out in L.A. and I watched people paralyze themselves with the pressure of: I’ve told people I’m a director so now I have to direct something that has to be good. If it’s not good, everyone’s going to judge me. The result of that thinking is two years on one short film.”
“You can’t put too much pressure on an idea,” adds Barker. “You just got to make it.”

item.title: 珍娜·布什·海格 (Jenna Bush Hager) 的版本现已在奥普拉读书俱乐部发布
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——奥普拉·温弗瑞（Oprah Winfrey）最新的读书俱乐部精选也得到了第二位文学时尚引领者珍娜·布什·哈格（Jenna Bush Hager）的祝福。
温弗瑞周二宣布，她选择了苏菲·陈·凯勒刚刚出版的《小奇迹》。凯勒的小说由海格千声出版社与企鹅兰登书屋合作发行。《小奇迹》以中国为背景，讲述了一名送餐员和她的天才儿子（音乐神童）在北京迷路的故事。
温弗瑞在一份声明中说：“我满怀希望地期待着这对母子这段难忘的旅程的结果。”“痛苦的经历和意想不到的善意使这本书成为一部值得纪念的小说。”
温弗瑞对凯勒的播客采访可以在奥普拉 YouTube 频道和其他播客网站上观看。海格是《Today》节目的联合主持人和“与詹娜一起阅读”读书俱乐部的创始人，他不仅出版了《小奇迹》，还向奥弗瑞寄去了一份早期副本，并促成了奥弗瑞在电话中告知作者她的决定。
“接到奥普拉的电话是一个令人惊讶的梦想成真，”凯勒在一份声明中说。“我仍然难以置信。但如果我能回到过去，告诉那个蜷缩在妈妈身边读睡前故事的小女孩，有一天奥普拉会读她的故事，并选择它参加她的读书俱乐部，这个小女孩会相信的——因为她知道世界是神奇的。”
凯勒是中国人，在加利福尼亚长大，与丈夫和两个孩子住在德国。她还是 2017 年出版的小说《失物的光泽》的作者。
